message.content:
<command-message>start-task is running…</command-message>
<command-name>/start-task</command-name>
<command-args>Welcome to a blank slate. A new project that you will help build from the ground up. This is for a company called "OakStone Properties" that we will be developing a mobile focus website for. I say mobile focused because most end users will be on mobile devices. The owner/admin I am building this for will mostly be viewing on desktop, but need to also do everything on either. First - we need to setup up this workspace with a CLAUDE.md file and a .claude folder with all the best tools, plugins, slash commands, skills, agents and anything else we can bring in that could potentially help. We will document everything. We will keep this workspace / codebase clean, lean, secure, encrypted, and provide a beautifully simple UX/UI as both the end users and owner are non-technical. They should never have to do anything more than the bare minimum for their roles in this project. I am thinking we should allow Google and/or Facebook SSO, while being able to browse the listings without signing in. The owner will not have GitHub or Cloudflare access - although I will purchase the domain and setup the repo in a Cloudflare and GitHub account that I setup for the owner, and just give myself full access to the project. Must be free in any circumstances possible, other than the domain yearly fee. ChatGPT 5.2 Prompt: I want to create a free (except for the yearly domain) website to host rental properties. I want the owner of the properties (not me, building this website for somebody) to have an admin portal to add / remove properties, pictures, information and have them hosted somewhere for free (GitHub?). The domain will be bought and deployed through Cloudflare. The build will be hosted on GitHub. I will connect the GitHub repo to Cloudflare. That is all I have worked out so far. The admin / owner should also be able to hide properties that are not available, and show properties that are and easily be able to change them at any time. We want this platform to look beautiful and clean. Easy to navigate for the end-users who will likely only have a mobile device - but it needs to look good on both desktop and mobile. Potential renters should be able to fill out an application and any other required forms, along with submit a payment when needed to the owner for processing applications and background checks and whatever else there may be. Please develop a full plan for me to provide to my engineer (Claude Code CLI). Research online for skills, subagents, plugins, slash commands and anything else that may be useful as well as doing deep research on the best way to setup a potential site like this using only free services or making it as cheap as possible in the long term. It must be easy to manage for the owner (non-technical) and it must be easy for potential renters to browse, apply, etc. (non-technical). ChatGPT 5.2 Ideas: Option A (recommended): Static site + Git-based admin + outsourced screening + Stripe for owner-collected fees
Why this is the best long-term “free/cheap” design

Fast, mobile-first, SEO-friendly static site on Cloudflare Pages with GitHub integration (push → automatic deploy). 
Cloudflare Docs
+2
Cloudflare Docs
+2

Non-technical admin portal using Decap CMS at /admin that edits content in the repo (add/remove properties, toggle available/unavailable, update photos/info). 
Decap CMS
+1

Avoid handling sensitive applicant data (SSN, credit, criminal checks) by sending applicants to a tenant-screening platform (e.g., RentSpree) that collects the data and fees directly from applicants. 
rentspree.com
+2
RentSpree Help Center
+2

Payments: if the owner needs to collect an application/admin fee, use Stripe Payment Links (no-code, pay per transaction, no monthly SaaS). 
Stripe Docs
+2
Stripe Docs
+2

Architecture
Hosting / DNS

Domain purchased annually → DNS hosted in Cloudflare → Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repo. 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Cloudflare Pages Free plan supports up to 500 builds/month (typical property sites won’t hit this). 
Cloudflare Docs

Frontend stack (static)

Astro (recommended) + Tailwind + component library (your choice)

Astro has a documented Decap CMS integration pattern and supports content collections for typed property data. 
Astro Docs

Build output deployed by Pages (standard npm run build + dist/). 
Cloudflare Docs

Admin portal (owner-friendly)

Decap CMS served from public/admin/ and accessible at https://yourdomain.com/admin/. 
Astro Docs

Owner workflow: login → “Properties” collection → Add/Edit/Hide/Publish → Decap commits to GitHub → Cloudflare rebuilds → site updates.

Decap authentication on Cloudflare Pages (important)

Decap’s GitHub backend needs a server-side OAuth flow; Decap notes Netlify facilitates this by default. 
Decap CMS

To keep everything on Cloudflare, use an external OAuth client (Decap supports this approach). 
Decap CMS

Practical implementation: deploy a small Cloudflare Worker OAuth proxy (template exists) and point Decap backend.base_url to it. 
GitHub

Data model (properties)

Represent each property as a file (Markdown or JSON) in the repo. Minimum fields:

id (uuid), slug

title (e.g., “2BR Townhome – Caledonia”)

status enum: available | pending | rented (drives hide/show)

rent (number), deposit (number)

beds, baths, sqft

addressPublic (optional masked), neighborhood, lat/lng (optional)

features[] (AC, parking, laundry, pets, etc.)

photos[] (paths or URLs)

applyUrl (RentSpree link or internal form route)

applicationFeePaymentLink (Stripe Payment Link URL if needed)

Photos storage guidance

Simplest: store images in repo under public/images/properties/... and let Decap upload there.

Avoid Git LFS: Decap GitHub backend does not support Git LFS, so keep images reasonably compressed. 
Decap CMS

If you expect heavy photo volume, move to Cloudflare R2 for object storage (still cheap/free-tier), but that requires either:

owner pastes R2 image URLs into Decap fields, or

you build a small “media uploader” page for the owner.

Applicant flow (browse → apply → pay)
Browsing UX (mobile-first)

Home: featured available listings + search/filter (beds/baths, max rent, pets, availability)

Listing detail: photo gallery, key facts, amenities, map area (optional), “Apply” + “Contact”

Clear CTA: “Apply now”, “Schedule a tour”, “Ask a question”

Accessibility: keyboard nav, readable contrast, big tap targets, lazy-loaded images

Applications and screening (recommended: outsource)

RentSpree approach:

“Apply” button takes applicant to RentSpree application + screening

Applicant pays screening fee directly (owner avoids collecting + storing sensitive info) 
RentSpree Help Center
+1

RentSpree offers rental application (free) and paid screening components. 
rentspree.com
+1

Application fee / other owner-collected payments

If the owner charges a fee separate from screening:

Use Stripe Payment Links for a no-code hosted checkout page. 
Stripe Docs
+2
Stripe Docs
+2

If you want a tighter integrated flow later:

Use Stripe Checkout (hosted page) created by a serverless endpoint (Pages Function). 
Stripe Docs
+2
Stripe Docs
+2

If you must host your own application form (Option A2)

You can still stay mostly “free” on Cloudflare:

Frontend form page posts to a Cloudflare Pages Function handler. 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Protect forms from bot spam using Cloudflare Turnstile (+ Pages plugin). 
Cloudflare Docs
+2
Cloudflare Docs
+2

Store submissions in Cloudflare D1 (serverless SQL). 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Upload supporting documents (pay stubs, IDs) into Cloudflare R2 and store object keys in D1; R2 has a published free tier. 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Send owner notifications via Cloudflare Email Routing / Worker email. 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Quota notes to design around:

Pages Functions requests count against Workers Free daily request cap (100k/day). 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

D1 free tier enforcement started Feb 10, 2025 (design to stay within daily free limits). 
Cloudflare Docs

Security warning: if you collect high-risk PII (SSN, DOB, full credit history), you become the data custodian. Avoid this unless there’s a business requirement.

Hardening / security checklist

Put /admin behind:

Decap auth (GitHub OAuth via Worker proxy), and optionally

Cloudflare Access policy allowing only the owner’s identity provider/email. 
Cloudflare Docs

Turnstile on every public form endpoint. 
Cloudflare Docs
+1

Rate limit / WAF rules for /api/* and /admin/* (optional)

Use environment secrets in Pages (OAuth client secret, Stripe secret key if using Checkout, Turnstile secret). 
Cloudflare Docs

Engineering implementation plan (what to hand your engineer)
Milestone 1 — Foundation (static site)

Create Astro project + Tailwind + base layout + routing

Implement property list + property detail pages from content collection/files

Implement availability filtering and “Hide rented” logic (status != available)

Add sitemap/robots, metadata, OpenGraph

Milestone 2 — Admin portal (Decap)

Add public/admin/index.html + config.yml

Define collections:

properties

site_pages (About, Contact, FAQ)

settings (application links, fee links, phone/email)

Implement previews (optional)

Implement GitHub OAuth via Cloudflare Worker proxy (decap-proxy pattern)

Add Cloudflare Access policy for /admin (optional but recommended)

Milestone 3 — Apply + Pay flows (outsourced-first)

Add per-property applyUrl and optional stripePaymentLink

UX: “Apply” opens RentSpree (or chosen provider) in-app browser safe

Add confirmation page + FAQ (“What happens after you apply?”)

Milestone 4 — (Optional) Native forms + storage

Pages Functions endpoint(s): /api/apply, /api/contact

Turnstile verification server-side

D1 schema:

applications, messages, audit_log

R2 uploads (signed upload URL flow)

Email notification integration

Milestone 5 — Polish

Lighthouse performance pass (image compression, lazy loading, caching)

Mobile navigation, gallery UX, sticky “Apply” CTA

Analytics (optional)

Claude Code CLI enablement (subagents, skills, slash commands, plugins, hooks)
Recommended structure in your repo

CLAUDE.md: project rules, commands, architecture notes (Claude Code explicitly recommends documenting requirements and using hooks). 
Claude Code

.claude/settings.json: hooks (format on write, run tests, block dangerous edits). 
Claude Code
+2
Claude Code
+2

.claude/commands/: custom slash commands (repeatable workflows). 
Claude Code
+1

.claude/agents/: subagents for focused roles (frontend, CF Workers, security review). 
Claude Code
+1

Optional shared skills under ~/.claude/skills/ or repo-based skill directories (for reusable build standards). 
Claude Code

Suggested subagents (create 4)

frontend-ui: component library, responsive layout, accessibility

cms-admin: Decap config modeling, editorial workflow

cf-worker-backend: Pages Functions, Turnstile verification, D1/R2 bindings

security-review: threat modeling, secrets, auth flows, abuse cases

(Subagents are explicitly designed as specialized assistants with separate context windows.) 
Claude Code

Suggested slash commands (project-scoped)

/spec: write feature spec + acceptance criteria

/cms-model: update Decap config.yml + validate collections

/cf-deploy-check: verify Pages build settings + env vars

/api-apply: generate Pages Function endpoint + Turnstile verification

/stripe-link: generate “owner uses Payment Link” UX text and admin field wiring

/sec-check: run a lightweight security checklist on diffs

(Custom slash commands are file-based and user-invoked.) 
Claude Code

Hooks (deterministic guardrails)

Use hooks to:

auto-run eslint/prettier after edits

prevent commits that include secrets

require updating tests when functions change
(Hooks are shell commands executed at lifecycle points like PreToolUse/PostToolUse.) 
Claude Code
+1

Plugins and marketplaces (team sharing)

If you want standardized behavior across engineers:

package commands/agents/hooks/skills into a plugin and distribute via a marketplace file. 
Claude Code
+2
Claude Code
+2

CLI usage notes

Claude Code CLI supports interactive sessions, “prompt then exit”, resume sessions, updates, and MCP server configuration. 
Claude Code
+1

Cost model (what will actually cost money)

Domain: yearly (your only guaranteed fixed cost)

Cloudflare Pages/Workers/D1/R2: can be $0 if you stay within free tiers (Pages builds, Workers requests, D1 limits, R2 free tier). 
Cloudflare Docs
+3
Cloudflare Docs
+3
Cloudflare Docs
+3

Stripe: per-transaction processing fees (no monthly for Payment Links). 
Stripe Docs
+1

Screening provider (if outsourced): applicant-paid fees are typical (example shown by RentSpree). 
RentSpree Help Center
+1

Option B (more complex): Full custom admin + database

If you don’t want the owner to have GitHub access at all:

Build an authenticated admin app (Cloudflare Access + Pages Functions)

Store properties in D1, images in R2

This becomes a real web app (more code, more security surface, more testing), but still can run on free/cheap Cloudflare primitives. 
Cloudflare Docs
+4
Cloudflare Docs
+4
Cloudflare Docs
+4

Option C (worst for “free forever”): SaaS property management platform

Fastest to deploy, but recurring monthly costs usually appear as soon as you need forms, screening integrations, or more listings.</command-args>

content.text:
# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes task and routes to optimal execution strategy.

## Routing Logic

**No task provided:** → `/check-todos`

**Task provided - analyze and route:**

| Keywords/Signals | Route To |
|-----------------|----------|
| "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase" | `/create-plan {{task}}` |
| "should we", "which", "evaluate", "compare" | `/consider:[framework] {{task}}` |
| "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "race condition" | `Skill(debug-like-expert)` |
| Moderate complexity (30min-2hr, 3-10 files) | `/create-prompt {{task}}` → `/run` |
| "research then", "explore then", multi-stage | `/create-meta-prompt {{task}}` |
| Simple (1-2 files, <30min, clear scope) | Execute directly |

## Decision Framework Selection

| Decision Type | Framework |
|---------------|-----------|
| Technical choice | `/consider:first-principles` |
| Priority/urgency | `/consider:eisenhower-matrix` |
| Consequences | `/consider:second-order` |
| Root cause | `/consider:5-whys` |

## Examples

```bash
/start-task Plan the auth system                    # → /create-plan
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or SSE?       # → /consider:first-principles
/start-task Debug intermittent widget failure      # → debug-like-expert
/start-task Refactor calendar components           # → /create-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL then implement        # → /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Fix typo in README                     # → direct execution
/start-task                                        # → /check-todos
```

input.prompt:
Explore the current state of the oakstone.properties workspace at D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\oakstone.properties

Determine:
1. Is this a completely empty directory or are there existing files?
2. Does a CLAUDE.md file exist?
3. Does a .claude folder exist?
4. Is there a package.json or any project configuration?
5. Any existing source code or content?

Also check the parent workspace CLAUDE.md files referenced at:
- D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\CLAUDE.md
- D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\.claude\CLAUDE.md
- C:\Users\tjn\.claude\CLAUDE.md

To understand what patterns and configurations are already established in the user's environment that we should follow for this new project.

Research the user's existing Claude Code configuration to understand what tools, plugins, skills, and subagents are already available that we can leverage for this project.

Explore:
1. C:\Users\tjn\.claude\skills\ - What skills exist?
2. C:\Users\tjn\.claude\plugins\ - What plugins are installed?
3. C:\Users\tjn\.claude\commands\ - What slash commands exist?
4. C:\Users\tjn\.claude\agents\ - Any existing subagent configurations?
5. C:\Users\tjn\.claude\settings.json - What hooks and permissions are configured?

Identify which of these existing resources would be most useful for a rental property website project with:
- Astro + Tailwind frontend
- Decap CMS for admin
- Cloudflare Pages deployment

# OakStone Properties - Project Initialization Plan

## Executive Summary

Initialize a new rental property website project for OakStone Properties with:
- **Mobile-first** static site on Astro + Tailwind
- **Non-technical admin** portal via Decap CMS
- **Free hosting** on Cloudflare Pages (GitHub-backed)
- **Outsourced screening** to avoid PII liability
- **Stripe Payment Links** for application fees

**Owner:** Non-technical, desktop primary, no GitHub/Cloudflare access
**End Users:** Non-technical, mobile primary, browsing/applying for rentals
**Developer:** You (Travis) - full access to GitHub/Cloudflare accounts you manage

---

## Phase 1: Workspace Initialization

### 1.1 Create CLAUDE.md
**File:** `oakstone.properties/CLAUDE.md`

Content structure:
```markdown
# OakStone Properties - Project Configuration

## Project Overview
- Company: OakStone Properties
- Type: Rental property listing website
- Stack: Astro + Tailwind + Decap CMS
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages (free tier)
- Domain: oakstoneproperties.com (or chosen domain)

## Target Users
- **End Users:** Mobile-first renters (non-technical)
- **Admin/Owner:** Desktop-primary property manager (non-technical)

## Tech Stack
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Astro 5.x |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS 4.x |
| CMS | Decap CMS (Git-based) |
| Hosting | Cloudflare Pages |
| CDN/DNS | Cloudflare |
| Forms | Cloudflare Turnstile + Pages Functions |
| Payments | Stripe Payment Links |
| Screening | RentSpree (outsourced) |
| Images | Cloudflare R2 (free tier) |

## Architecture Principles
1. **Static-first** - Pre-build all property pages
2. **Mobile-first** - 320px minimum, progressive enhancement
3. **Offline-capable** - Service worker for cached listings
4. **Zero PII storage** - Outsource sensitive data collection
5. **Git-based content** - Properties as Markdown files

## Performance Targets
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Lighthouse Performance | 95+ |
| LCP | <2.5s |
| FID | <100ms |
| CLS | <0.1 |
| Bundle size | <50KB gzipped |

## Security Requirements
- [ ] Turnstile on all public forms
- [ ] No PII in codebase or D1
- [ ] Secrets in Cloudflare environment variables
- [ ] Admin behind Cloudflare Access
- [ ] No innerHTML usage

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Touch targets >= 48px (mobile)
- [ ] Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- [ ] Keyboard navigation
- [ ] Screen reader support
- [ ] Focus indicators
```

### 1.2 Create .claude Directory Structure
```
oakstone.properties/.claude/
├── settings.json          # Project-specific hooks/permissions
├── commands/              # Custom slash commands
│   ├── property.md        # Add/edit property workflow
│   ├── deploy.md          # Build and deploy check
│   ├── cms-sync.md        # Validate Decap config
│   └── seo-check.md       # SEO audit for listings
├── agents/                # Specialized subagents
│   ├── frontend-ui.md     # Component/layout specialist
│   ├── cms-admin.md       # Decap CMS specialist
│   ├── cf-worker.md       # Cloudflare Functions specialist
│   └── security.md        # Security review specialist
└── prompts/               # Reusable task prompts
    └── 001-initial-setup.md
```

### 1.3 Project Slash Commands

**`/property`** - Property content workflow
```markdown
# Add/Edit Property

Steps:
1. Validate property data schema
2. Create/update Markdown file in content/properties/
3. Optimize and place images in public/images/properties/
4. Update Decap CMS preview
5. Test property page rendering
```

**`/deploy`** - Deployment verification
```markdown
# Deploy Check

Steps:
1. Run npm run build
2. Verify no TypeScript errors
3. Check Lighthouse scores
4. Validate sitemap.xml
5. Confirm Cloudflare Pages settings
```

**`/cms-sync`** - CMS configuration
```markdown
# CMS Sync

Steps:
1. Validate config.yml against schema
2. Check all collections have required fields
3. Verify preview templates work
4. Test OAuth flow
```

**`/seo-check`** - SEO audit
```markdown
# SEO Check

Steps:
1. Validate meta tags on all pages
2. Check Open Graph images
3. Verify Schema.org PropertyListing markup
4. Confirm robots.txt and sitemap.xml
5. Test Core Web Vitals
```

### 1.4 Project Subagents

**`frontend-ui`**
- Focus: Astro components, Tailwind styling, responsive layouts
- Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Mobile-first design, accessibility, performance

**`cms-admin`**
- Focus: Decap CMS configuration, content modeling, editorial UX
- Tools: Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Git-based CMS, Markdown schemas, preview templates

**`cf-worker`**
- Focus: Cloudflare Pages Functions, Turnstile, D1/R2 bindings
- Tools: Bash, Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Edge functions, serverless, Cloudflare ecosystem

**`security`**
- Focus: Auth flows, secrets management, input validation
- Tools: Grep, Read
- Expertise: OWASP, Cloudflare Access, threat modeling

---

## Phase 2: Project Scaffolding

### 2.1 Initialize Astro Project
```bash
npm create astro@latest oakstone.properties -- --template minimal
cd oakstone.properties
npm install
```

### 2.2 Add Dependencies
```bash
# Styling
npm install -D tailwindcss @astrojs/tailwind

# CMS
npm install decap-cms-app

# Cloudflare
npm install -D @cloudflare/workers-types wrangler

# Image optimization
npm install -D @astrojs/image sharp

# SEO
npm install astro-seo astro-robots-txt @astrojs/sitemap
```

### 2.3 Directory Structure
```
oakstone.properties/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/
│   │   │   ├── Header.astro
│   │   │   ├── Footer.astro
│   │   │   ├── Navigation.astro
│   │   │   └── MobileNav.astro
│   │   ├── property/
│   │   │   ├── PropertyCard.astro
│   │   │   ├── PropertyGallery.astro
│   │   │   ├── PropertyDetails.astro
│   │   │   └── PropertyFilters.astro
│   │   ├── forms/
│   │   │   ├── ContactForm.astro
│   │   │   └── ApplicationButton.astro
│   │   └── ui/
│   │       ├── Button.astro
│   │       ├── Card.astro
│   │       └── Badge.astro
│   ├── layouts/
│   │   ├── BaseLayout.astro
│   │   └── PropertyLayout.astro
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── index.astro          # Homepage with featured listings
│   │   ├── properties/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro      # All properties with filters
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro     # Individual property page
│   │   ├── about.astro
│   │   ├── contact.astro
│   │   ├── apply.astro          # Application info page
│   │   └── faq.astro
│   ├── content/
│   │   ├── config.ts            # Content collections schema
│   │   └── properties/          # Property Markdown files
│   │       └── example-property.md

├── public/

│   └── favicon.svg
├── functions/                    # Cloudflare Pages Functions
│   └── api/
│       ├── contact.ts           # Contact form handler
│       └── _middleware.ts       # Turnstile verification
├── astro.config.mjs
├── tailwind.config.mjs
├── package.json
├── CLAUDE.md
└── .claude/
```

### 2.4 Property Content Schema
```typescript
// src/content/config.ts
import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content';

const properties = defineCollection({
  type: 'content',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    slug: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['available', 'pending', 'rented']),
    featured: z.boolean().default(false),

    // Pricing
    rent: z.number(),
    deposit: z.number(),

    // Property details
    beds: z.number(),
    baths: z.number(),
    sqft: z.number().optional(),
    propertyType: z.enum(['apartment', 'house', 'townhome', 'condo', 'duplex']),

    // Location (masked for privacy)
    neighborhood: z.string(),
    city: z.string(),
    state: z.string().length(2),
    zipCode: z.string().optional(),

    // Features
    features: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    petPolicy: z.enum(['no-pets', 'cats-only', 'dogs-only', 'cats-dogs', 'negotiable']).default('no-pets'),
    parking: z.enum(['none', 'street', 'lot', 'garage', 'covered']).default('none'),
    laundry: z.enum(['none', 'shared', 'in-unit', 'hookups']).default('none'),

    // Media
    photos: z.array(z.string()),
    virtualTourUrl: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Application
    applyUrl: z.string().url(),
    applicationFee: z.number().optional(),
    stripePaymentLink: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Dates
    availableDate: z.date().optional(),
    publishedDate: z.date(),
    updatedDate: z.date().optional(),
  }),
});

export const collections = { properties };
```

---

## Phase 3: Decap CMS Integration

### 3.1 CMS Configuration
```yaml
# public/admin/config.yml
backend:
  name: github
  repo: owner/oakstone-properties
  branch: main
  base_url: https://your-oauth-proxy.workers.dev  # Cloudflare Worker OAuth

media_folder: public/images/properties
public_folder: /images/properties

collections:
  - name: properties
    label: Properties
    folder: src/content/properties
    create: true
    slug: "{{slug}}"
    fields:
      - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
      - { name: slug, label: URL Slug, widget: string }
      - { name: status, label: Status, widget: select, options: [available, pending, rented] }
      - { name: featured, label: Featured, widget: boolean, default: false }
      - { name: rent, label: Monthly Rent, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: deposit, label: Security Deposit, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: beds, label: Bedrooms, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: baths, label: Bathrooms, widget: number, value_type: float }
      - { name: sqft, label: Square Feet, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: propertyType, label: Property Type, widget: select, options: [apartment, house, townhome, condo, duplex] }
      - { name: neighborhood, label: Neighborhood, widget: string }
      - { name: city, label: City, widget: string }
      - { name: state, label: State, widget: string }
      - { name: features, label: Features, widget: list }
      - { name: petPolicy, label: Pet Policy, widget: select, options: [no-pets, cats-only, dogs-only, cats-dogs, negotiable] }
      - { name: parking, label: Parking, widget: select, options: [none, street, lot, garage, covered] }
      - { name: laundry, label: Laundry, widget: select, options: [none, shared, in-unit, hookups] }
      - { name: photos, label: Photos, widget: list, field: { name: photo, widget: image } }
      - { name: applyUrl, label: Application URL, widget: string }
      - { name: applicationFee, label: Application Fee, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: stripePaymentLink, label: Stripe Payment Link, widget: string, required: false }
      - { name: availableDate, label: Available Date, widget: datetime, required: false }
      - { name: publishedDate, label: Published Date, widget: datetime }
      - { name: body, label: Description, widget: markdown }

  - name: pages
    label: Pages
    files:
      - name: about
        label: About Page
        file: src/content/pages/about.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: body, label: Content, widget: markdown }
      - name: faq
        label: FAQ
        file: src/content/pages/faq.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: questions, label: Questions, widget: list, fields: [
              { name: question, label: Question, widget: string },
              { name: answer, label: Answer, widget: markdown }
            ]}

  - name: settings
    label: Site Settings
    files:
      - name: contact
        label: Contact Information
        file: src/content/settings/contact.json
        fields:
          - { name: phone, label: Phone, widget: string }
          - { name: email, label: Email, widget: string }
          - { name: address, label: Office Address, widget: text, required: false }
          - { name: hours, label: Office Hours, widget: string }
```

### 3.2 OAuth Proxy Worker
Deploy a Cloudflare Worker to handle GitHub OAuth for Decap CMS:
- Use `decap-server-cloudflare-worker` template
- Configure OAuth app in GitHub
- Point Decap `base_url` to Worker URL

---

## Phase 4: Authentication & Payments

### 4.1 Admin Access (Cloudflare Access)
- Create Cloudflare Access application for `/admin/*`
- Allow only owner's email (Google/email OTP)
- No GitHub account needed for owner

### 4.2 End User Auth (Browse without login)
- No auth required for browsing listings
- Optional: Google/Facebook SSO for saved favorites (future)
- Application flows go to RentSpree (external auth)

### 4.3 Payment Integration
- Stripe Payment Links for application fees
- Owner creates links in Stripe Dashboard
- Links stored in property content via Decap CMS
- No Stripe code needed in site

---

## Phase 5: Forms & Serverless

### 5.1 Contact Form
```typescript
// functions/api/contact.ts
import { verifyTurnstile } from '../_middleware';

export async function onRequestPost({ request, env }) {
  const formData = await request.formData();
  const turnstileToken = formData.get('cf-turnstile-response');

  // Verify Turnstile
  const isValid = await verifyTurnstile(turnstileToken, env.TURNSTILE_SECRET);
  if (!isValid) {
    return new Response('Invalid captcha', { status: 403 });
  }

  // Send email via Cloudflare Email
  // Or store in D1 for owner dashboard

  return new Response('Message sent', { status: 200 });
}
```

### 5.2 Turnstile Integration
- Add Turnstile widget to contact form
- Verify server-side in Pages Function
- Free unlimited verifications

---

## Phase 6: SEO & Performance

### 6.1 SEO Implementation
- Schema.org `RentalListing` markup on property pages
- Open Graph images auto-generated
- Dynamic sitemap with all properties
- robots.txt allowing all crawlers
- Local SEO optimization for property locations

### 6.2 Performance Optimization
- Image optimization via Sharp
- Lazy loading for photo galleries
- Preload critical assets
- Service worker for offline browsing
- Cloudflare caching headers

---

## Decision Points (Questions for User)

1. **Domain:** What domain has been/will be purchased?

2. **Authentication for Owner:**
   - Option A: Cloudflare Access with Google/email OTP (simplest)
   - Option B: GitHub OAuth (requires owner GitHub account)
   - Recommendation: Option A

3. **Application Flow:**
   - Option A: RentSpree (outsourced, applicant pays screening)
   - Option B: Custom form + D1 storage (you handle data)
   - Recommendation: Option A initially, Option B as enhancement

4. **Image Storage:**
   - Option A: Git repo (simple, <100MB recommended)
   - Option B: Cloudflare R2 (scalable, free tier 10GB)
   - Recommendation: Start with A, migrate to B if needed

5. **Branding:**
   - Primary brand color?
   - Logo available?
   - Any existing brand guidelines?

6. **Content:**
   - How many properties to start?
   - Any existing property data to migrate?

---

## Implementation Order

### Milestone 1: Foundation (Phase 1-2)
- [ ] Create CLAUDE.md and .claude/ structure
- [ ] Initialize Astro project with Tailwind
- [ ] Setup content collections and property schema
- [ ] Create base layouts and navigation

### Milestone 2: Property Listings (Phase 2)
- [ ] Build PropertyCard component
- [ ] Build PropertyDetails page
- [ ] Implement filtering/search
- [ ] Add sample property data

### Milestone 3: Admin Portal (Phase 3)
- [ ] Configure Decap CMS
- [ ] Deploy OAuth proxy Worker
- [ ] Test content editing workflow
- [ ] Write admin documentation

### Milestone 4: Forms & Integration (Phase 4-5)
- [ ] Implement contact form with Turnstile
- [ ] Configure Cloudflare Access for /admin
- [ ] Setup RentSpree integration
- [ ] Add Stripe Payment Link fields

### Milestone 5: Polish & Deploy (Phase 6)
- [ ] SEO implementation
- [ ] Performance optimization
- [ ] Lighthouse audit
- [ ] Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
- [ ] Connect domain

---

## Files to Create (Summary)

**Workspace Setup:**
1. `oakstone.properties/CLAUDE.md`
2. `oakstone.properties/.claude/settings.json`
3. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/property.md`
4. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/deploy.md`
5. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/cms-sync.md`
6. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/seo-check.md`
7. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/frontend-ui.md`
8. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cms-admin.md`
9. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cf-worker.md`
10. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/security.md`

**Then project scaffolding as outlined in Phase 2.**

---

## Cost Summary

| Service | Cost |
|---------|------|
| Domain | ~$10-15/year |
| Cloudflare Pages | Free |
| Cloudflare Workers | Free (100k req/day) |
| Cloudflare D1 | Free (within limits) |
| Cloudflare R2 | Free (10GB storage) |
| Cloudflare Access | Free (50 users) |
| Cloudflare Turnstile | Free |
| GitHub | Free (public/private repos) |
| Stripe | ~2.9% + $0.30 per payment |
| RentSpree | Applicant-paid |
| **Total Ongoing** | **~$10-15/year + payment fees** |

```typescript
const properties = defineCollection({
  type: 'content',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    slug: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['available', 'pending', 'rented']),
    featured: z.boolean().default(false),

    // Pricing
    rent: z.number(),
    deposit: z.number(),

    // Property details
    beds: z.number(),
    baths: z.number(),
    sqft: z.number().optional(),
    propertyType: z.enum(['apartment', 'house', 'townhome', 'condo', 'duplex']),

    // Location (masked for privacy)
    neighborhood: z.string(),
    city: z.string(),
    state: z.string().length(2),
    zipCode: z.string().optional(),

    // Features
    features: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    petPolicy: z.enum(['no-pets', 'cats-only', 'dogs-only', 'cats-dogs', 'negotiable']).default('no-pets'),
    parking: z.enum(['none', 'street', 'lot', 'garage', 'covered']).default('none'),
    laundry: z.enum(['none', 'shared', 'in-unit', 'hookups']).default('none'),

    // Media
    photos: z.array(z.string()),
    virtualTourUrl: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Application
    applyUrl: z.string().url(),
    applicationFee: z.number().optional(),
    stripePaymentLink: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Dates
    availableDate: z.date().optional(),
    publishedDate: z.date(),
    updatedDate: z.date().optional(),
  }),
});

export const collections = { properties };
```

---

## Phase 3: Decap CMS Integration

### 3.1 CMS Configuration
```yaml
# public/admin/config.yml
backend:
  name: github
  repo: owner/oakstone-properties
  branch: main
  base_url: https://your-oauth-proxy.workers.dev  # Cloudflare Worker OAuth

media_folder: public/images/properties
public_folder: /images/properties

collections:
  - name: properties
    label: Properties
    folder: src/content/properties
    create: true
    slug: "{{slug}}"
    fields:
      - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
      - { name: slug, label: URL Slug, widget: string }
      - { name: status, label: Status, widget: select, options: [available, pending, rented] }
      - { name: featured, label: Featured, widget: boolean, default: false }
      - { name: rent, label: Monthly Rent, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: deposit, label: Security Deposit, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: beds, label: Bedrooms, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: baths, label: Bathrooms, widget: number, value_type: float }
      - { name: sqft, label: Square Feet, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: propertyType, label: Property Type, widget: select, options: [apartment, house, townhome, condo, duplex] }
      - { name: neighborhood, label: Neighborhood, widget: string }
      - { name: city, label: City, widget: string }
      - { name: state, label: State, widget: string }
      - { name: features, label: Features, widget: list }
      - { name: petPolicy, label: Pet Policy, widget: select, options: [no-pets, cats-only, dogs-only, cats-dogs, negotiable] }
      - { name: parking, label: Parking, widget: select, options: [none, street, lot, garage, covered] }
      - { name: laundry, label: Laundry, widget: select, options: [none, shared, in-unit, hookups] }
      - { name: photos, label: Photos, widget: list, field: { name: photo, widget: image } }
      - { name: applyUrl, label: Application URL, widget: string }
      - { name: applicationFee, label: Application Fee, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: stripePaymentLink, label: Stripe Payment Link, widget: string, required: false }
      - { name: availableDate, label: Available Date, widget: datetime, required: false }
      - { name: publishedDate, label: Published Date, widget: datetime }
      - { name: body, label: Description, widget: markdown }

  - name: pages
    label: Pages
    files:
      - name: about
        label: About Page
        file: src/content/pages/about.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: body, label: Content, widget: markdown }
      - name: faq
        label: FAQ
        file: src/content/pages/faq.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: questions, label: Questions, widget: list, fields: [
              { name: question, label: Question, widget: string },
              { name: answer, label: Answer, widget: markdown }
            ]}

  - name: settings
    label: Site Settings
    files:
      - name: contact
        label: Contact Information
        file: src/content/settings/contact.json
        fields:
          - { name: phone, label: Phone, widget: string }
          - { name: email, label: Email, widget: string }
          - { name: address, label: Office Address, widget: text, required: false }
          - { name: hours, label: Office Hours, widget: string }
```

### 3.2 OAuth Proxy Worker
Deploy a Cloudflare Worker to handle GitHub OAuth for Decap CMS:
- Use `decap-server-cloudflare-worker` template
- Configure OAuth app in GitHub
- Point Decap `base_url` to Worker URL

---

## Phase 4: Authentication & Payments

### 4.1 Admin Access (Cloudflare Access)
- Create Cloudflare Access application for `/admin/*`
- Allow only owner's email (Google/email OTP)
- No GitHub account needed for owner

### 4.2 End User Auth (Browse without login)
- No auth required for browsing listings
- Optional: Google/Facebook SSO for saved favorites (future)
- Application flows go to RentSpree (external auth)

### 4.3 Payment Integration
- Stripe Payment Links for application fees
- Owner creates links in Stripe Dashboard
- Links stored in property content via Decap CMS
- No Stripe code needed in site

---

## Phase 5: Forms & Serverless

### 5.1 Contact Form
```typescript
// functions/api/contact.ts
import { verifyTurnstile } from '../_middleware';

export async function onRequestPost({ request, env }) {
  const formData = await request.formData();
  const turnstileToken = formData.get('cf-turnstile-response');

  // Verify Turnstile
  const isValid = await verifyTurnstile(turnstileToken, env.TURNSTILE_SECRET);
  if (!isValid) {
    return new Response('Invalid captcha', { status: 403 });
  }

  // Send email via Cloudflare Email
  // Or store in D1 for owner dashboard

  return new Response('Message sent', { status: 200 });
}
```

### 5.2 Turnstile Integration
- Add Turnstile widget to contact form
- Verify server-side in Pages Function
- Free unlimited verifications

---

## Phase 6: SEO & Performance

### 6.1 SEO Implementation
- Schema.org `RentalListing` markup on property pages
- Open Graph images auto-generated
- Dynamic sitemap with all properties
- robots.txt allowing all crawlers
- Local SEO optimization for property locations

### 6.2 Performance Optimization
- Image optimization via Sharp
- Lazy loading for photo galleries
- Preload critical assets
- Service worker for offline browsing
- Cloudflare caching headers

---

## Decision Points (Questions for User)

1. **Domain:** What domain has been/will be purchased?

2. **Authentication for Owner:**
   - Option A: Cloudflare Access with Google/email OTP (simplest)
   - Option B: GitHub OAuth (requires owner GitHub account)
   - Recommendation: Option A

3. **Application Flow:**
   - Option A: RentSpree (outsourced, applicant pays screening)
   - Option B: Custom form + D1 storage (you handle data)
   - Recommendation: Option A initially, Option B as enhancement

4. **Image Storage:**
   - Option A: Git repo (simple, <100MB recommended)
   - Option B: Cloudflare R2 (scalable, free tier 10GB)
   - Recommendation: Start with A, migrate to B if needed

5. **Branding:**
   - Primary brand color?
   - Logo available?
   - Any existing brand guidelines?

6. **Content:**
   - How many properties to start?
   - Any existing property data to migrate?

---

## Implementation Order

### Milestone 1: Foundation (Phase 1-2)
- [ ] Create CLAUDE.md and .claude/ structure
- [ ] Initialize Astro project with Tailwind
- [ ] Setup content collections and property schema
- [ ] Create base layouts and navigation

### Milestone 2: Property Listings (Phase 2)
- [ ] Build PropertyCard component
- [ ] Build PropertyDetails page
- [ ] Implement filtering/search
- [ ] Add sample property data

### Milestone 3: Admin Portal (Phase 3)
- [ ] Configure Decap CMS
- [ ] Deploy OAuth proxy Worker
- [ ] Test content editing workflow
- [ ] Write admin documentation

### Milestone 4: Forms & Integration (Phase 4-5)
- [ ] Implement contact form with Turnstile
- [ ] Configure Cloudflare Access for /admin
- [ ] Setup RentSpree integration
- [ ] Add Stripe Payment Link fields

### Milestone 5: Polish & Deploy (Phase 6)
- [ ] SEO implementation
- [ ] Performance optimization
- [ ] Lighthouse audit
- [ ] Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
- [ ] Connect domain

---

## Files to Create (Summary)

**Workspace Setup:**
1. `oakstone.properties/CLAUDE.md`
2. `oakstone.properties/.claude/settings.json`
3. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/property.md`
4. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/deploy.md`
5. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/cms-sync.md`
6. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/seo-check.md`
7. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/frontend-ui.md`
8. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cms-admin.md`
9. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cf-worker.md`
10. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/security.md`

**Then project scaffolding as outlined in Phase 2.**

---

## Cost Summary

| Service | Cost |
|---------|------|
| Domain | ~$10-15/year |
| Cloudflare Pages | Free |
| Cloudflare Workers | Free (100k req/day) |
| Cloudflare D1 | Free (within limits) |
| Cloudflare R2 | Free (10GB storage) |
| Cloudflare Access | Free (50 users) |
| Cloudflare Turnstile | Free |
| GitHub | Free (public/private repos) |
| Stripe | ~2.9% + $0.30 per payment |
| RentSpree | Applicant-paid |
| **Total Ongoing** | **~$10-15/year + payment fees** |

content.text:
Excellent questions. Let me address these and update the plan:

**Domain:** Will use `TBD` in the plan - owner decides later.

**Auth:** Google SSO for admin + Google/Facebook SSO for end users who want to save favorites.

**Applications:** Will document as a decision point for owner.

**Hosting concerns:** This is a critical question. Let me clarify:

**GitHub vs Cloudflare Reality:**
- **GitHub Pages** is 100% free but has 1GB repo limit and 100MB file size limit
- **Cloudflare Pages** is also 100% free with generous limits:
  - 500 builds/month
  - 25MB max file size per asset
  - No storage limit
  - The "billing horror stories" are from **Cloudflare Workers/R2** with uncapped usage, NOT Pages

**Recommended approach:** Use **Cloudflare Pages** (truly free, no surprise bills for a static site) with **GitHub repo** (for version control and Decap CMS backend). Images stay in the repo under 25MB each - just compress them. This setup has no metered billing.

Let me update the plan with these answers:

## Executive Summary

Initialize a new rental property website project for OakStone Properties with:
- **Mobile-first** static site on Astro + Tailwind
- **Non-technical admin** portal via Decap CMS
- **Free hosting** on Cloudflare Pages (GitHub-backed)
- **Google SSO** for admin access (no GitHub account needed)
- **Google/Facebook SSO** for end users (optional, for favorites)
- **Stripe Payment Links** for application fees
- **Application flow** TBD by owner (RentSpree vs custom)

**Owner:** Non-technical, desktop primary, no GitHub/Cloudflare access, has Google account
**End Users:** Non-technical, mobile primary, browsing/applying for rentals
**Developer:** You (Travis) - full access to GitHub/Cloudflare accounts you manage
**Domain:** TBD - owner will purchase

---

## Hosting Costs Clarification

**Why this setup is safe from surprise bills:**

| Service | Cost Model | Risk |
|---------|------------|------|
| **Cloudflare Pages** | 100% free (500 builds/mo) | ZERO - no metered billing |
| **GitHub** | Free (public or private repos) | ZERO |
| **Cloudflare Workers** | Free tier 100k req/day | Low risk - contact form only |
| **Cloudflare D1** | Free tier limits | Low risk - small data |
| **Cloudflare R2** | AVOID unless needed | This is where billing surprises happen |

**Strategy:** Keep images in Git repo (<25MB each, compressed). Avoid R2 unless portfolio exceeds 1GB of images. For a typical 10-50 property site, Git repo storage is sufficient.

**Horror story prevention:** Cloudflare's billing issues come from:
1. R2 storage with high bandwidth (we won't use R2)
2. Workers with uncapped API calls (we'll use minimal workers)
3. Enterprise features accidentally enabled (we use free tier only)

A static site on Pages with occasional Pages Functions has **no metered billing whatsoever**.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# OakStone Properties - Project Initialization Plan

## Executive Summary

Initialize a new rental property website project for OakStone Properties with:
- **Mobile-first** static site on Astro + Tailwind
- **Non-technical admin** portal via Decap CMS
- **Free hosting** on Cloudflare Pages (GitHub-backed)
- **Outsourced screening** to avoid PII liability
- **Stripe Payment Links** for application fees

**Owner:** Non-technical, desktop primary, no GitHub/Cloudflare access
**End Users:** Non-technical, mobile primary, browsing/applying for rentals
**Developer:** You (Travis) - full access to GitHub/Cloudflare accounts you manage

---

## Phase 1: Workspace Initialization

### 1.1 Create CLAUDE.md
**File:** `oakstone.properties/CLAUDE.md`

Content structure:
```markdown
# OakStone Properties - Project Configuration

## Project Overview
- Company: OakStone Properties
- Type: Rental property listing website
- Stack: Astro + Tailwind + Decap CMS
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages (free tier)
- Domain: oakstoneproperties.com (or chosen domain)

## Target Users
- **End Users:** Mobile-first renters (non-technical)
- **Admin/Owner:** Desktop-primary property manager (non-technical)

## Tech Stack
| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Framework | Astro 5.x |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS 4.x |
| CMS | Decap CMS (Git-based) |
| Hosting | Cloudflare Pages |
| CDN/DNS | Cloudflare |
| Forms | Cloudflare Turnstile + Pages Functions |
| Payments | Stripe Payment Links |
| Screening | RentSpree (outsourced) |
| Images | Cloudflare R2 (free tier) |

## Architecture Principles
1. **Static-first** - Pre-build all property pages
2. **Mobile-first** - 320px minimum, progressive enhancement
3. **Offline-capable** - Service worker for cached listings
4. **Zero PII storage** - Outsource sensitive data collection
5. **Git-based content** - Properties as Markdown files

## Performance Targets
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Lighthouse Performance | 95+ |
| LCP | <2.5s |
| FID | <100ms |
| CLS | <0.1 |
| Bundle size | <50KB gzipped |

## Security Requirements
- [ ] Turnstile on all public forms
- [ ] No PII in codebase or D1
- [ ] Secrets in Cloudflare environment variables
- [ ] Admin behind Cloudflare Access
- [ ] No innerHTML usage

## Accessibility (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Touch targets >= 48px (mobile)
- [ ] Color contrast >= 4.5:1
- [ ] Keyboard navigation
- [ ] Screen reader support
- [ ] Focus indicators
```

### 1.2 Create .claude Directory Structure
```
oakstone.properties/.claude/
├── settings.json          # Project-specific hooks/permissions
├── commands/              # Custom slash commands
│   ├── property.md        # Add/edit property workflow
│   ├── deploy.md          # Build and deploy check
│   ├── cms-sync.md        # Validate Decap config
│   └── seo-check.md       # SEO audit for listings
├── agents/                # Specialized subagents
│   ├── frontend-ui.md     # Component/layout specialist
│   ├── cms-admin.md       # Decap CMS specialist
│   ├── cf-worker.md       # Cloudflare Functions specialist
│   └── security.md        # Security review specialist
└── prompts/               # Reusable task prompts
    └── 001-initial-setup.md
```

### 1.3 Project Slash Commands

**`/property`** - Property content workflow
```markdown
# Add/Edit Property

Steps:
1. Validate property data schema
2. Create/update Markdown file in content/properties/
3. Optimize and place images in public/images/properties/
4. Update Decap CMS preview
5. Test property page rendering
```

**`/deploy`** - Deployment verification
```markdown
# Deploy Check

Steps:
1. Run npm run build
2. Verify no TypeScript errors
3. Check Lighthouse scores
4. Validate sitemap.xml
5. Confirm Cloudflare Pages settings
```

**`/cms-sync`** - CMS configuration
```markdown
# CMS Sync

Steps:
1. Validate config.yml against schema
2. Check all collections have required fields
3. Verify preview templates work
4. Test OAuth flow
```

**`/seo-check`** - SEO audit
```markdown
# SEO Check

Steps:
1. Validate meta tags on all pages
2. Check Open Graph images
3. Verify Schema.org PropertyListing markup
4. Confirm robots.txt and sitemap.xml
5. Test Core Web Vitals
```

### 1.4 Project Subagents

**`frontend-ui`**
- Focus: Astro components, Tailwind styling, responsive layouts
- Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Mobile-first design, accessibility, performance

**`cms-admin`**
- Focus: Decap CMS configuration, content modeling, editorial UX
- Tools: Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Git-based CMS, Markdown schemas, preview templates

**`cf-worker`**
- Focus: Cloudflare Pages Functions, Turnstile, D1/R2 bindings
- Tools: Bash, Read, Edit, Write
- Expertise: Edge functions, serverless, Cloudflare ecosystem

**`security`**
- Focus: Auth flows, secrets management, input validation
- Tools: Grep, Read
- Expertise: OWASP, Cloudflare Access, threat modeling

---

## Phase 2: Project Scaffolding

### 2.1 Initialize Astro Project
```bash
npm create astro@latest oakstone.properties -- --template minimal
cd oakstone.properties
npm install
```

### 2.2 Add Dependencies
```bash
# Styling
npm install -D tailwindcss @astrojs/tailwind

# CMS
npm install decap-cms-app

# Cloudflare
npm install -D @cloudflare/workers-types wrangler

# Image optimization
npm install -D @astrojs/image sharp

# SEO
npm install astro-seo astro-robots-txt @astrojs/sitemap
```

### 2.3 Directory Structure
```
oakstone.properties/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/
│   │   │   ├── Header.astro
│   │   │   ├── Footer.astro
│   │   │   ├── Navigation.astro
│   │   │   └── MobileNav.astro
│   │   ├── property/
│   │   │   ├── PropertyCard.astro
│   │   │   ├── PropertyGallery.astro
│   │   │   ├── PropertyDetails.astro
│   │   │   └── PropertyFilters.astro
│   │   ├── forms/
│   │   │   ├── ContactForm.astro
│   │   │   └── ApplicationButton.astro
│   │   └── ui/
│   │       ├── Button.astro
│   │       ├── Card.astro
│   │       └── Badge.astro
│   ├── layouts/
│   │   ├── BaseLayout.astro
│   │   └── PropertyLayout.astro
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── index.astro          # Homepage with featured listings
│   │   ├── properties/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro      # All properties with filters
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro     # Individual property page
│   │   ├── about.astro
│   │   ├── contact.astro
│   │   ├── apply.astro          # Application info page
│   │   └── faq.astro
│   ├── content/
│   │   ├── config.ts            # Content collections schema
│   │   └── properties/          # Property Markdown files
│   │       └── example-property.md
│   └── styles/
│       └── global.css           # Tailwind + custom properties
├── public/
│   ├── admin/
│   │   ├── index.html           # Decap CMS entry
│   │   └── config.yml           # Decap CMS configuration
│   ├── images/
│   │   └── properties/          # Property photos
│   └── favicon.svg
├── functions/                    # Cloudflare Pages Functions
│   └── api/
│       ├── contact.ts           # Contact form handler
│       └── _middleware.ts       # Turnstile verification
├── astro.config.mjs
├── tailwind.config.mjs
├── package.json
├── CLAUDE.md
└── .claude/
```

### 2.4 Property Content Schema
```typescript
// src/content/config.ts
import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content';

const properties = defineCollection({
  type: 'content',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    slug: z.string(),
    status: z.enum(['available', 'pending', 'rented']),
    featured: z.boolean().default(false),

    // Pricing
    rent: z.number(),
    deposit: z.number(),

    // Property details
    beds: z.number(),
    baths: z.number(),
    sqft: z.number().optional(),
    propertyType: z.enum(['apartment', 'house', 'townhome', 'condo', 'duplex']),

    // Location (masked for privacy)
    neighborhood: z.string(),
    city: z.string(),
    state: z.string().length(2),
    zipCode: z.string().optional(),

    // Features
    features: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    petPolicy: z.enum(['no-pets', 'cats-only', 'dogs-only', 'cats-dogs', 'negotiable']).default('no-pets'),
    parking: z.enum(['none', 'street', 'lot', 'garage', 'covered']).default('none'),
    laundry: z.enum(['none', 'shared', 'in-unit', 'hookups']).default('none'),

    // Media
    photos: z.array(z.string()),
    virtualTourUrl: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Application
    applyUrl: z.string().url(),
    applicationFee: z.number().optional(),
    stripePaymentLink: z.string().url().optional(),

    // Dates
    availableDate: z.date().optional(),
    publishedDate: z.date(),
    updatedDate: z.date().optional(),
  }),
});

export const collections = { properties };
```

---

## Phase 3: Decap CMS Integration

### 3.1 CMS Configuration
```yaml
# public/admin/config.yml
backend:
  name: github
  repo: owner/oakstone-properties
  branch: main
  base_url: https://your-oauth-proxy.workers.dev  # Cloudflare Worker OAuth

media_folder: public/images/properties
public_folder: /images/properties

collections:
  - name: properties
    label: Properties
    folder: src/content/properties
    create: true
    slug: "{{slug}}"
    fields:
      - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
      - { name: slug, label: URL Slug, widget: string }
      - { name: status, label: Status, widget: select, options: [available, pending, rented] }
      - { name: featured, label: Featured, widget: boolean, default: false }
      - { name: rent, label: Monthly Rent, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: deposit, label: Security Deposit, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: beds, label: Bedrooms, widget: number, value_type: int }
      - { name: baths, label: Bathrooms, widget: number, value_type: float }
      - { name: sqft, label: Square Feet, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: propertyType, label: Property Type, widget: select, options: [apartment, house, townhome, condo, duplex] }
      - { name: neighborhood, label: Neighborhood, widget: string }
      - { name: city, label: City, widget: string }
      - { name: state, label: State, widget: string }
      - { name: features, label: Features, widget: list }
      - { name: petPolicy, label: Pet Policy, widget: select, options: [no-pets, cats-only, dogs-only, cats-dogs, negotiable] }
      - { name: parking, label: Parking, widget: select, options: [none, street, lot, garage, covered] }
      - { name: laundry, label: Laundry, widget: select, options: [none, shared, in-unit, hookups] }
      - { name: photos, label: Photos, widget: list, field: { name: photo, widget: image } }
      - { name: applyUrl, label: Application URL, widget: string }
      - { name: applicationFee, label: Application Fee, widget: number, required: false }
      - { name: stripePaymentLink, label: Stripe Payment Link, widget: string, required: false }
      - { name: availableDate, label: Available Date, widget: datetime, required: false }
      - { name: publishedDate, label: Published Date, widget: datetime }
      - { name: body, label: Description, widget: markdown }

  - name: pages
    label: Pages
    files:
      - name: about
        label: About Page
        file: src/content/pages/about.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: body, label: Content, widget: markdown }
      - name: faq
        label: FAQ
        file: src/content/pages/faq.md
        fields:
          - { name: title, label: Title, widget: string }
          - { name: questions, label: Questions, widget: list, fields: [
              { name: question, label: Question, widget: string },
              { name: answer, label: Answer, widget: markdown }
            ]}

  - name: settings
    label: Site Settings
    files:
      - name: contact
        label: Contact Information
        file: src/content/settings/contact.json
        fields:
          - { name: phone, label: Phone, widget: string }
          - { name: email, label: Email, widget: string }
          - { name: address, label: Office Address, widget: text, required: false }
          - { name: hours, label: Office Hours, widget: string }
```

### 3.2 OAuth Proxy Worker
Deploy a Cloudflare Worker to handle GitHub OAuth for Decap CMS:
- Use `decap-server-cloudflare-worker` template
- Configure OAuth app in GitHub
- Point Decap `base_url` to Worker URL

---

## Phase 4: Authentication & Payments

### 4.1 Admin Access (Cloudflare Access)
- Create Cloudflare Access application for `/admin/*`
- Allow only owner's email (Google/email OTP)
- No GitHub account needed for owner

### 4.2 End User Auth (Browse without login)
- No auth required for browsing listings
- Optional: Google/Facebook SSO for saved favorites (future)
- Application flows go to RentSpree (external auth)

### 4.3 Payment Integration
- Stripe Payment Links for application fees
- Owner creates links in Stripe Dashboard
- Links stored in property content via Decap CMS
- No Stripe code needed in site

---

## Phase 5: Forms & Serverless

### 5.1 Contact Form
```typescript
// functions/api/contact.ts
import { verifyTurnstile } from '../_middleware';

export async function onRequestPost({ request, env }) {
  const formData = await request.formData();
  const turnstileToken = formData.get('cf-turnstile-response');

  // Verify Turnstile
  const isValid = await verifyTurnstile(turnstileToken, env.TURNSTILE_SECRET);
  if (!isValid) {
    return new Response('Invalid captcha', { status: 403 });
  }

  // Send email via Cloudflare Email
  // Or store in D1 for owner dashboard

  return new Response('Message sent', { status: 200 });
}
```

### 5.2 Turnstile Integration
- Add Turnstile widget to contact form
- Verify server-side in Pages Function
- Free unlimited verifications

---

## Phase 6: SEO & Performance

### 6.1 SEO Implementation
- Schema.org `RentalListing` markup on property pages
- Open Graph images auto-generated
- Dynamic sitemap with all properties
- robots.txt allowing all crawlers
- Local SEO optimization for property locations

### 6.2 Performance Optimization
- Image optimization via Sharp
- Lazy loading for photo galleries
- Preload critical assets
- Service worker for offline browsing
- Cloudflare caching headers

---

## Decision Points (Questions for User)

1. **Domain:** What domain has been/will be purchased?

2. **Authentication for Owner:**
   - Option A: Cloudflare Access with Google/email OTP (simplest)
   - Option B: GitHub OAuth (requires owner GitHub account)
   - Recommendation: Option A

3. **Application Flow:**
   - Option A: RentSpree (outsourced, applicant pays screening)
   - Option B: Custom form + D1 storage (you handle data)
   - Recommendation: Option A initially, Option B as enhancement

4. **Image Storage:**
   - Option A: Git repo (simple, <100MB recommended)
   - Option B: Cloudflare R2 (scalable, free tier 10GB)
   - Recommendation: Start with A, migrate to B if needed

5. **Branding:**
   - Primary brand color?
   - Logo available?
   - Any existing brand guidelines?

6. **Content:**
   - How many properties to start?
   - Any existing property data to migrate?

---

## Implementation Order

### Milestone 1: Foundation (Phase 1-2)
- [ ] Create CLAUDE.md and .claude/ structure
- [ ] Initialize Astro project with Tailwind
- [ ] Setup content collections and property schema
- [ ] Create base layouts and navigation

### Milestone 2: Property Listings (Phase 2)
- [ ] Build PropertyCard component
- [ ] Build PropertyDetails page
- [ ] Implement filtering/search
- [ ] Add sample property data

### Milestone 3: Admin Portal (Phase 3)
- [ ] Configure Decap CMS
- [ ] Deploy OAuth proxy Worker
- [ ] Test content editing workflow
- [ ] Write admin documentation

### Milestone 4: Forms & Integration (Phase 4-5)
- [ ] Implement contact form with Turnstile
- [ ] Configure Cloudflare Access for /admin
- [ ] Setup RentSpree integration
- [ ] Add Stripe Payment Link fields

### Milestone 5: Polish & Deploy (Phase 6)
- [ ] SEO implementation
- [ ] Performance optimization
- [ ] Lighthouse audit
- [ ] Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
- [ ] Connect domain

---

## Files to Create (Summary)

**Workspace Setup:**
1. `oakstone.properties/CLAUDE.md`
2. `oakstone.properties/.claude/settings.json`
3. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/property.md`
4. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/deploy.md`
5. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/cms-sync.md`
6. `oakstone.properties/.claude/commands/seo-check.md`
7. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/frontend-ui.md`
8. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cms-admin.md`
9. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/cf-worker.md`
10. `oakstone.properties/.claude/agents/security.md`

**Then project scaffolding as outlined in Phase 2.**

---

## Cost Summary

| Service | Cost |
|---------|------|
| Domain | ~$10-15/year |
| Cloudflare Pages | Free |
| Cloudflare Workers | Free (100k req/day) |
| Cloudflare D1 | Free (within limits) |
| Cloudflare R2 | Free (10GB storage) |
| Cloudflare Access | Free (50 users) |
| Cloudflare Turnstile | Free |
| GitHub | Free (public/private repos) |
| Stripe | ~2.9% + $0.30 per payment |
| RentSpree | Applicant-paid |
| **Total Ongoing** | **~$10-15/year + payment fees** |

## Phase 4: Authentication & Payments

### 4.1 Admin Access (Cloudflare Access + Google SSO)
- Create Cloudflare Access application for `/admin/*`
- Configure Google as identity provider
- Allow only owner's Google email
- Owner logs in with Google account - no GitHub needed
- Decap CMS authenticates via Cloudflare Access (not GitHub OAuth)

**Alternative Admin Auth (if Google doesn't work):**
Use Decap's GitHub OAuth via Cloudflare Worker proxy - but owner would need a GitHub account.

### 4.2 End User Auth (Optional - for favorites/saved searches)
**Browse without login:** Yes - all property browsing is public

**Optional authenticated features:**
- Save favorite properties
- Save search preferences
- Get email alerts for new listings

**Implementation:**
- Cloudflare Access with Google + Facebook identity providers
- Store preferences in browser localStorage (no D1 needed)
- Email alerts via Cloudflare Email Workers (phase 2 enhancement)

### 4.3 Payment Integration
- Stripe Payment Links for application fees
- Owner creates links in Stripe Dashboard (no code)
- Links stored in property content via Decap CMS
- No Stripe API integration needed

### 4.4 Application Flow (OWNER DECISION NEEDED)

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| **A: RentSpree** | Zero PII liability, professional screening, applicant pays | Less control, external redirect |
| **B: Custom Forms + D1** | Full control, integrated UX | You store PII, security responsibility |
| **C: Launch simple, decide later** | Fastest to MVP | Delays application flow |

**Recommendation:** Start with Option C (contact form only), then add Option A (RentSpree) when owner is ready. Avoid Option B unless there's a specific business requirement.
